","type":"text/x-markdown"},"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/runner/README.md":{"title":"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/runner/README.md","text":"# runner

> Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine via HTTP route

Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine via an HTTP route and trigger them from the browser with an action widget.

## Key features

* **Whitelist security** -- only commands defined in `runner-actions.json` can be executed
* **Parameterized commands** -- `{{placeholder}}` templates substituted from query params
* **Action widget** -- `<$api.run action="name" param="value"/>` for use inside `<$button>`
* **HTTP route** -- `GET /api/run?action=<name>` for direct invocation

## Prerequisites

* Commands must be configured in `runner-actions.json` (next to `tiddlywiki.info`)

## Quick start

Add an action entry to `runner-actions.json`:

```json
{ "myaction": { "command": "echo hello", "description": "Test" } }
```

Use in any tiddler:

```html
<$button><$api.run action="myaction"/>Run</$button>
```

## Plugin Library

Install from the [rimir plugin library](https://rimir-cc.github.io/tw-plugin-library/) via *Control Panel → Plugins → Get more plugins*.

## License

MIT -- see [LICENSE.md](LICENSE.md)

|!Version |!Date |!Changes |
|v0.1.4 |2026-04-01 |Document extraction route (`/api/extract?uri=`) — resolves `_canonical_uri` to filesystem path (supports `files/` and scattered-binaries profiles), runs pdftotext/pandoc/python, returns markdown text. 30s timeout, 10MB buffer. XLSX helper script (`scripts/xlsx-to-md.py`). `child_process.exec` now uses `cwd: $tw.boot.wikiPath` for correct working directory |
|v0.1.3 |2026-03-27 |Fix dev-workflow doc: remove incorrect version history, link to changelog tab |
|v0.1.2 |2026-03-15 |Add regression tests and CI test gate |
|v0.1.1 |2026-03-06 |Standardize plugin tabs: add documentation/changelog/license to list, replace license.tid with LICENSE.md.meta sidecar |
|v0.1.0 |2026 |Initial release |
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/architecture":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/architecture","created":"20260305110721405","modified":"20260305110721405","rdt.order":"2","rdt.plugin-key":"runner","rdt.plugin-name":"runner","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine","rdt.section":"Architecture","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Data flow

# Browser: user clicks a `<$button>` containing `<$api.run action="X" param="Y"/>`
# The `<$api.run>` action widget builds a URL and dispatches a `tm-http-request` message
# Request reaches the server: `GET /api/run?action=X&param=Y`
# `route.js` receives the request and looks up the action name in the whitelist loaded from `runner-actions.json`
# If the action exists, `{{placeholder}}` tokens in the command template are substituted with the matching query parameters
# The resolved command is executed via `child_process.exec()`
# The server responds with a JSON object: `{status, output}`

!! Components

|!Component |!Layer |!Role |
|`route.js` |Server (Node.js) |HTTP route handler — validates action, substitutes params, executes command |
|`widget.js` |Browser |`<$api.run>` action widget — builds URL, dispatches `tm-http-request` |
|`runner-actions.json` |Filesystem |Whitelist of allowed actions with command templates |

!! Design decisions

* ''Whitelist-only'': no arbitrary command execution — only actions defined in `runner-actions.json` can run
* ''Filesystem config'': the whitelist lives on the filesystem, not in the wiki store, creating a security boundary that prevents browser-side editing
* ''Parameter whitelisting'': only params explicitly listed in the action's `params` array are substituted into the command template; unlisted query params are ignored
* ''`{{placeholder}}` template syntax'': simple token replacement for parameterized commands
* ''Widget export convention'': `exports["api.run"]` causes TiddlyWiki to register the tag `<$api.run>`
* ''Fire-and-forget pattern'': the widget dispatches the HTTP request without handling the return value

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="warning">
<$fill $name="body">
No shell metacharacter escaping is performed. This is safe because the server is local-only and commands are owner-defined in the whitelist.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/dev-workflow":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/dev-workflow","created":"20260305110721406","modified":"20260305110721406","rdt.order":"3","rdt.plugin-key":"runner","rdt.plugin-name":"runner","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine","rdt.section":"Dev Workflow","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Plugin files

|!File |!Role |
|`plugin.info` |Plugin metadata |
|`route.js` |Server-side HTTP route (`module-type: route`) |
|`widget.js` |Browser-side action widget (`module-type: widget`) |
|`runner-actions.json` |Command whitelist (filesystem, not a plugin file) |

!! Restart rules

|!Change |!Restart needed? |
|Edit `runner-actions.json` |Yes — read from disk at boot |
|Edit `route.js` |Yes — JS module |
|Edit `widget.js` |Yes — JS module |
|Edit `.tid` files |Yes — plugin shadow tiddlers |

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="tip">
<$fill $name="body">
On Windows, use `taskkill //F //PID <pid> //T` to kill the server.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

!! Adding a new action

# Edit `runner-actions.json` in the wiki root
# Add an action entry with `command`, `description`, and an optional `params` array
# Restart the server

!! Testing

* Via curl: `curl "http://localhost:8080/api/run?action=calc"`
* Via wiki: add a `<$button>` with `<$api.run action="..."/>` inside

!! Version history

See the [[changelog|$:/plugins/rimir/runner/changelog]] tab for the full version history.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/examples":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/examples","created":"20260305110721406","modified":"20260305110721406","rdt.order":"4","rdt.plugin-key":"runner","rdt.plugin-name":"runner","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine","rdt.section":"Examples","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Basic usage — Open Calculator

```
<$button>
  Open Calculator
  <$api.run action="calc"/>
</$button>
```

!! Parameterized commands — Echo a message

```
<$button>
  Say Hello
  <$api.run action="echo" msg="Hello from TiddlyWiki"/>
</$button>
```

!! Action definition format

The `runner-actions.json` file maps action names to command definitions:

```json
{
  "action-name": {
    "command": "some-command {{param1}} --flag {{param2}}",
    "description": "Human-readable description",
    "params": ["param1", "param2"]
  }
}
```

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
<$fill $name="body">
Only params listed in the action's `params` array are substituted into the command template. Unlisted params in the URL are ignored.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

!! Multiple actions in one tiddler

You can place several buttons in a single tiddler to build a command dashboard:

```
<$button>
  Open Calculator
  <$api.run action="calc"/>
</$button>

<$button>
  Greet
  <$api.run action="echo" msg="Hello from TiddlyWiki"/>
</$button>
```

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/faq":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/faq","created":"20260305110721407","modified":"20260305110721407","rdt.order":"5","rdt.plugin-key":"runner","rdt.plugin-name":"runner","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine","rdt.section":"FAQ","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Q: Is this safe to expose on a network?

No. The runner executes real commands on the host machine. It is designed for local development only. If you expose the TiddlyWiki server to a network, anyone can trigger whitelisted commands via `GET /api/run`.

!! Q: Why is the config a filesystem file, not a wiki tiddler?

Security boundary. Wiki tiddlers can be edited from the browser, which would let anyone with browser access modify the command whitelist. Keeping `runner-actions.json` on the filesystem means only someone with file system access can change which commands are allowed.

!! Q: Can I chain multiple commands?

Yes, using shell syntax in the command template. For example: `"command": "echo start && do-something && echo done"`. The entire string is passed to `child_process.exec()` which runs it in a shell.

!! Q: What if a command hangs?

There is no built-in timeout. A hanging command will keep the HTTP connection open indefinitely. The browser's `tm-http-request` may eventually time out on the client side, but the server process will still be running. Kill it manually if needed.

!! Q: How do I pass complex arguments with spaces?

Use shell quoting in the command template. For example: `"command": "echo \"{{msg}}\""`. The `{{placeholder}}` substitution is a simple string replace before the command reaches the shell.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/overview":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/overview","created":"20260305110721407","modified":"20260305110721407","rdt.hub-category":"infrastructure","rdt.hub-category-name":"Infrastructure","rdt.hub-order":"1","rdt.order":"1","rdt.plugin-key":"runner","rdt.plugin-name":"runner","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine","rdt.section":"Overview","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! What it does

Runner lets you execute pre-defined commands on the host machine from within TiddlyWiki. Commands are defined in a JSON whitelist file on the filesystem — only listed actions can be executed.

!! Features

* ''Whitelist-only execution'' — only commands defined in `runner-actions.json` can run
* ''Parameterized commands'' — `{{placeholder}}` templates substituted from query parameters
* ''Browser widget'' — `<$api.run action="X" param="Y"/>` action widget for use in buttons
* ''HTTP API'' — `GET /api/run?action=<name>` endpoint
* ''Filesystem config'' — whitelist lives outside the wiki store (not editable from browser)

!! Prerequisites

* TiddlyWiki running in Node.js server mode
* `runner-actions.json` file in the wiki root (next to `tiddlywiki.info`)

!! Quick start

# Create `runner-actions.json` in your wiki root:

```json
{
  "calc": {
    "command": "calc",
    "description": "Open Calculator"
  },
  "echo": {
    "command": "echo {{msg}}",
    "description": "Echo a message",
    "params": ["msg"]
  }
}
```

# Restart the TiddlyWiki server

# Use in wiki markup:

```
<$button>
  Open Calculator
  <$api.run action="calc"/>
</$button>

<$button>
  Say Hello
  <$api.run action="echo" msg="Hello World"/>
</$button>
```

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
<$fill $name="body">
Changes to `runner-actions.json` require a server restart — the file is read from disk at boot time.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/reference":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/reference","created":"20260305110721408","modified":"20260305110721408","rdt.order":"6","rdt.plugin-key":"runner","rdt.plugin-name":"runner","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine","rdt.section":"Reference","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! GET /api/run

|!Parameter |!Required |!Description |
|`action` |Yes |Action name — must match a key in `runner-actions.json` |
|//any// |No |Additional params substituted into `{{placeholder}}` tokens |

Response (200):

```json
{"action": "echo", "status": "ok", "output": "Hello"}
```

Error responses:

|!HTTP |!Condition |
|400 |`action` parameter missing |
|403 |Action not found in whitelist |
|200 |Command execution error (status `"error"`, `error` field set) |

!! <$api.run> widget

|!Attribute |!Required |!Description |
|`action` |Yes |Action name passed as `?action=` |
|//any other// |No |Forwarded as query parameters (non-`$`-prefixed) |

Usage: must be inside a `<$button>` — it's an action widget.

```
<$button>
  Label
  <$api.run action="name" param1="value"/>
</$button>
```

!! runner-actions.json schema

```json
{
  "action-name": {
    "command": "string — shell command with optional {{param}} placeholders",
    "params": ["param1", "param2"],
    "description": "string — human-readable description"
  }
}
```

|!Field |!Required |!Description |
|`command` |Yes |Shell command template |
|`params` |No |Whitelist of allowed placeholder names |
|`description` |No |Human-readable label |

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/tw-internals":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/tw-internals","created":"20260305110721408","modified":"20260305110721408","rdt.order":"7","rdt.plugin-key":"runner","rdt.plugin-name":"runner","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine","rdt.section":"TW Internals","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! child_process.exec

The route uses Node's `child_process.exec()` to run commands. This spawns a shell (`/bin/sh` or `cmd.exe`) and passes the full command string. Stdout and stderr are combined in the response.

!! tm-http-request message

The `<$api.run>` widget dispatches `tm-http-request` — TiddlyWiki's built-in mechanism for browser-to-server HTTP calls. The widget does not use `XMLHttpRequest` directly; it delegates to TW's action message system which handles the actual XHR.

!! Action widget pattern

`exports["api.run"]` in `widget.js` causes TW to register the custom tag `<$api.run>`. The export key (with the dot) becomes the tag name with a `$` prefix. The widget extends `Widget` and implements `invokeAction()` which fires on button click.

!! Route module-type

`module-type: route` tells TW to register the module as an HTTP route handler. The module exports:

* `method` — HTTP method (`"GET"`)
* `path` — regex for URL matching (`/^\/api\/run$/`)
* `handler(request, response, state)` — request handler function

TW's HTTP server matches incoming requests against all registered routes.

!! sendJson helper

A common pattern across plugins: set `Content-Type: application/json`, optionally `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`, then `response.end(JSON.stringify(data))`.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/documentation":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/documentation","created":"20260305110721408","modified":"20260305110721408","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<%if [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]has[plugin-type]] %>
<$tiddler tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/overview">
<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/runner/doc/overview"/>
</$tiddler>
<%else%>
To display this documentation, the plugin [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]] needs to be installed.

See the [[readme|$:/plugins/rimir/runner/readme]] tab for a basic overview.
<%endif%>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/extract-route.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/extract-route.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/rimir/runner/extract-route.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: route

Server-side document extraction route.
GET /api/extract?uri=<canonical_uri>
Resolves the URI to a filesystem path, runs pandoc/python, returns extracted text.

Supports:
- /files/* — TiddlyWiki's built-in file serving (maps to <wikiPath>/files/*)
- Scattered-binaries routes — resolved via $:/config/rimir/scattered-binaries/profiles

\*/

"use strict";

var child_process = require("child_process");
var fs = require("fs");
var path = require("path");

var EXTRACTORS = {
	".pdf":  "pdftotext -layout \"{input}\" -",
	".docx": "pandoc \"{input}\" -t markdown --wrap=none",
	".xlsx": "python3 scripts/xlsx-to-md.py \"{input}\""
};

// Load scattered-binaries profiles at boot for URI-to-path resolution
var scatteredProfiles = [];
try {
	var profilesText = $tw.wiki.getTiddlerText("$:/config/rimir/scattered-binaries/profiles") || "[]";
	scatteredProfiles = JSON.parse(profilesText);
} catch(e) { /* no profiles */ }

exports.method = "GET";
exports.path = /^\/api\/extract$/;

exports.handler = function(request, response, state) {
	var uri = state.queryParameters.uri;
	if(!uri) {
		sendJson(response, 400, {status: "error", error: "Missing 'uri' parameter"});
		return;
	}

	var ext = path.extname(uri).toLowerCase();
	var commandTemplate = EXTRACTORS[ext];
	if(!commandTemplate) {
		sendJson(response, 400, {status: "error", error: "Unsupported file type: " + ext});
		return;
	}

	// Resolve URI to filesystem path
	var filePath = resolveUri(uri);
	if(!filePath) {
		sendJson(response, 400, {status: "error", error: "Cannot resolve URI to filesystem path: " + uri});
		return;
	}

	// Security: verify file exists and path doesn't escape wiki root
	var absPath = path.resolve(filePath);
	var wikiRoot = path.resolve($tw.boot.wikiPath);
	if(absPath.indexOf(wikiRoot) !== 0) {
		sendJson(response, 403, {status: "error", error: "Path traversal denied"});
		return;
	}

	if(!fs.existsSync(absPath)) {
		sendJson(response, 404, {status: "error", error: "File not found: " + absPath});
		return;
	}

	// Run extraction
	var command = commandTemplate.replace("{input}", absPath);
	child_process.exec(command, {cwd: $tw.boot.wikiPath, maxBuffer: 10 * 1024 * 1024, timeout: 30000}, function(err, stdout, stderr) {
		if(err) {
			sendJson(response, 200, {
				status: "error",
				error: err.message,
				output: (stdout || "") + (stderr || "")
			});
		} else {
			sendJson(response, 200, {
				status: "ok",
				output: (stdout || "") + (stderr || "")
			});
		}
	});
};

/*
Resolve a _canonical_uri to a filesystem path.
Handles:
- /files/* -> <wikiPath>/files/*
- Scattered-binaries routes -> profile basePath + dirName + subFolder + filePath
*/
function resolveUri(uri) {
	var decoded = decodeURIComponent(uri);

	// Simple case: /files/* maps directly to <wikiPath>/files/*
	if(decoded.indexOf("/files/") === 0) {
		return path.resolve($tw.boot.wikiPath, decoded.substring(1));
	}

	// Try scattered-binaries profiles
	for(var i = 0; i < scatteredProfiles.length; i++) {
		var profile = scatteredProfiles[i];
		var prefix = profile.routePrefix;
		if(!prefix || decoded.indexOf(prefix + "/") !== 0) continue;

		// URI format: /<routePrefix>/<dirName>/<filePath>
		var remainder = decoded.substring(prefix.length + 1);
		var slashIdx = remainder.indexOf("/");
		if(slashIdx === -1) continue;

		var dirName = remainder.substring(0, slashIdx);
		var filePath = remainder.substring(slashIdx + 1);

		// Filesystem: <basePath>/<dirName>/<subFolder>/<filePath>
		return path.resolve($tw.boot.wikiPath, profile.basePath, dirName, profile.subFolder, filePath);
	}

	// Fallback: treat as relative to wiki path (strip leading slash)
	if(decoded.charAt(0) === "/") decoded = decoded.substring(1);
	return path.resolve($tw.boot.wikiPath, decoded);
}

function sendJson(response, statusCode, data) {
	var body = JSON.stringify(data);
	response.writeHead(statusCode, {
		"Content-Type": "application/json"
	});
	response.end(body);
}
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"route"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/icon":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/icon","created":"20260305110721409","modified":"20260305110721409","type":"image/svg+xml","text":"<svg width="22pt" height="22pt" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="maroon" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">

Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine via an HTTP route and trigger them from the browser with an action widget.

!! Key features

* ''Whitelist security'' — only commands defined in `runner-actions.json` can be executed
* ''Parameterized commands'' — `{{placeholder}}` templates substituted from query params
* ''Action widget'' — `<$api.run action="name" param="value"/>` for use inside `<$button>`
* ''HTTP route'' — `GET /api/run?action=<name>` for direct invocation

!! Prerequisites

* Commands must be configured in `runner-actions.json` (next to `tiddlywiki.info`)

!! Quick start

Add an action entry to `runner-actions.json`:

```
{ "myaction": { "command": "echo hello", "description": "Test" } }
```

Use in any tiddler:

```
<$button><$api.run action="myaction"/>Run</$button>
```
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/route.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/route.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/rimir/runner/route.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: route

HTTP route that executes whitelisted commands on the host machine.
GET /run?action=<action_name>

\*/

"use strict";

var child_process = require("child_process");
var fs = require("fs");
var path = require("path");

// Read whitelist from filesystem config at boot — not editable from browser
var configPath = path.resolve($tw.boot.wikiPath, "runner-actions.json");
var actions = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(configPath, "utf8"));

exports.method = "GET";
exports.path = /^\/api\/run$/;

exports.handler = function(request, response, state) {
	var action = state.queryParameters.action;
	if(!action) {
		sendJson(response, 400, {error: "Missing 'action' query parameter"});
		return;
	}
	var entry = actions[action];
	if(!entry || !entry.command) {
		sendJson(response, 403, {error: "Unknown action: " + action});
		return;
	}
	// Substitute whitelisted parameters into command template
	var command = entry.command;
	var allowedParams = entry.params || [];
	for(var i = 0; i < allowedParams.length; i++) {
		var paramName = allowedParams[i];
		var paramValue = state.queryParameters[paramName] || "";
		command = command.split("{{" + paramName + "}}").join(paramValue);
	}
	child_process.exec(command, {cwd: $tw.boot.wikiPath}, function(err, stdout, stderr) {
		if(err) {
			sendJson(response, 200, {
				action: action,
				status: "error",
				error: err.message,
				output: (stdout || "") + (stderr || "")
			});
		} else {
			sendJson(response, 200, {
				action: action,
				status: "ok",
				output: (stdout || "") + (stderr || "")
			});
		}
	});
};

function sendJson(response, statusCode, data) {
	var body = JSON.stringify(data);
	response.writeHead(statusCode, {
		"Content-Type": "application/json",
		"Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*"
	});
	response.end(body);
}
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"route","created":"20260305110721409","modified":"20260305110721409"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/settings":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/settings","created":"20260305110721409","modified":"20260305110721409","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<div class="rr-settings-info-card">

"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/showcase":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/showcase","caption":"Runner Showcase","created":"20260305110721409","modified":"20260305110721409","tags":"","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! Runner Plugin Showcase

!! Open Calculator

<$button>
<$api.run action="calc"/>
Open Calculator
</$button>

!! Echo a Message

<$let msg="hello world">
<$button>
<$api.run action="echo" msg=<<msg>>/>
Echo: <<msg>>
</$button>
</$let>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/test/test-widget.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/test/test-widget.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/rimir/runner/test/test-widget.js
type: application/javascript
tags: [[$:/tags/test-spec]]

Tests for runner widget URL construction logic.

\*/
"use strict";

describe("runner: widget URL construction", function() {

	// Test the URL building logic directly, without needing the full widget dispatch chain.

	it("should build basic URL with action parameter", function() {
		var url = "/api/run?action=" + encodeURIComponent("calc");
		expect(url).toBe("/api/run?action=calc");
	});

	it("should encode action with special characters", function() {
		var url = "/api/run?action=" + encodeURIComponent("my action");
		expect(url).toBe("/api/run?action=my%20action");
	});

	it("should append non-reserved attributes as query params", function() {
		var attrs = {action: "echo", msg: "hello", count: "5"};
		var url = "/api/run?action=" + encodeURIComponent(attrs.action);
		for (var key in attrs) {
			if (key !== "action" && key.charAt(0) !== "$") {
				url += "&" + encodeURIComponent(key) + "=" + encodeURIComponent(attrs[key]);
			}
		}
		expect(url).toContain("action=echo");
		expect(url).toContain("msg=hello");
		expect(url).toContain("count=5");
	});

	it("should exclude $-prefixed attributes", function() {
		var attrs = {action: "test", "$class": "hidden", msg: "ok"};
		var url = "/api/run?action=" + encodeURIComponent(attrs.action);
		for (var key in attrs) {
			if (key !== "action" && key.charAt(0) !== "$") {
				url += "&" + encodeURIComponent(key) + "=" + encodeURIComponent(attrs[key]);
			}
		}
		expect(url).toContain("msg=ok");
		expect(url).not.toContain("class");
		expect(url).not.toContain("hidden");
	});

	it("should encode special characters in param values", function() {
		var val = "hello world&foo=bar";
		var encoded = encodeURIComponent(val);
		expect(encoded).toBe("hello%20world%26foo%3Dbar");
	});

	it("should handle empty action", function() {
		var url = "/api/run?action=" + encodeURIComponent("");
		expect(url).toBe("/api/run?action=");
	});

	// Test widget module exports
	it("should export api.run widget", function() {
		var widgetModule = require("$:/plugins/rimir/runner/widget.js");
		expect(widgetModule["api.run"]).toBeDefined();
		expect(typeof widgetModule["api.run"]).toBe("function");
	});

	it("should have invokeAction method", function() {
		var widgetModule = require("$:/plugins/rimir/runner/widget.js");
		expect(typeof widgetModule["api.run"].prototype.invokeAction).toBe("function");
	});
});
","type":"application/javascript","tags":"[[$:/tags/test-spec]]"},"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/runner/test-edition/tiddlywiki.info":{"title":"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/runner/test-edition/tiddlywiki.info","text":"{
    "description": "Test edition for runner",

        "tiddlywiki/jasmine",
        "rimir/runner"
    ],
    "themes": [
        "tiddlywiki/vanilla",
        "tiddlywiki/snowwhite"
    ]
}
","type":".info"},"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/widget.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/runner/widget.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/rimir/runner/widget.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget

Action widget that calls the runner API route.
<$api.run action="calc"/> or <$api.run action="echo" msg="hello"/>

\*/

"use strict";

var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

var ApiRunWidget = function(parseTreeNode, options) {
	this.initialise(parseTreeNode, options);
};

ApiRunWidget.prototype = new Widget();

ApiRunWidget.prototype.render = function(parent, nextSibling) {
	this.computeAttributes();
	this.execute();
	this.renderChildren(parent, nextSibling);
};

ApiRunWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
	this.actionName = this.getAttribute("action");
	this.makeChildWidgets();
};

ApiRunWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers) {
	var changedAttributes = this.computeAttributes();
	if(changedAttributes["action"]) {
		this.refreshSelf();
		return true;
	}
	return this.refreshChildren(changedTiddlers);
};

ApiRunWidget.prototype.invokeAction = function(triggeringWidget, event) {
	var url = "/api/run?action=" + encodeURIComponent(this.actionName || "");
	// Collect non-reserved attributes as query parameters
	var attributes = this.attributes;
	for(var key in attributes) {
		if(key !== "action" && key.charAt(0) !== "$") {
			url += "&" + encodeURIComponent(key) + "=" + encodeURIComponent(attributes[key]);
		}
	}
	this.dispatchEvent({
		type: "tm-http-request",
		param: url,
		paramObject: {
			method: "GET",
			url: url
		}
	});
	return true;
};

exports["api.run"] = ApiRunWidget;
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260305110721409","modified":"20260305110721409"}}}
